---
title: "Student Weekly Report"
author: "EduVision System"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
params:
  student_name: "Student Name"
  student_number: "STU-XXXX"
  program: "Program Name"
  year_of_study: 1
  week_display: "Week X, Year"
  week_start: "2024-01-01"
  week_end: "2024-01-07"
  weekly_attendance: data.frame()
  emotion_trends: data.frame()
  concentration_trends: data.frame()
  presence_data: data.frame()
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)

emotion_colors <- c(
  "happy" = "#10b981",
  "sad" = "#3b82f6",
  "angry" = "#ef4444",
  "fearful" = "#f59e0b",
  "surprised" = "#06b6d4",
  "neutral" = "#6b7280",
  "confused" = "#8b5cf6"
)

## Attendance helper
render_attendance_table <- function(wa) {
  if (is.null(wa) || nrow(wa) == 0) {
    cat("**No attendance data available for this week.**\n")
    return()
  }

  # Normalize column names used by generator
  # Expecting columns: course_name, sessions_attended, sessions_held, status (weekly status)
  course_col <- ifelse('course_name' %in% names(wa), 'course_name', ifelse('course' %in% names(wa), 'course', names(wa)[1]))
  sess_att_col <- ifelse('sessions_attended' %in% names(wa), 'sessions_attended', 'sessions_attended')
  sess_held_col <- ifelse('sessions_held' %in% names(wa), 'sessions_held', 'sessions_held')
  status_col <- ifelse('status' %in% names(wa), 'status', ifelse('weekly_status' %in% names(wa), 'weekly_status', 'status'))

  wa$display_status <- apply(wa, 1, function(row) {
    sa <- as.numeric(row[[sess_att_col]])
    ws <- tolower(as.character(row[[status_col]]))
    if (!is.na(sa) && sa > 0) {
      '✅ PRESENT (attended at least once)'
    } else if (!is.na(ws) && ws == 'excused') {
      '🟠 EXCUSED (approved absence)'
    } else {
      '❌ ABSENT (no attendance this week)'
    }
  })

  df <- data.frame(
    Course = wa[[course_col]],
    Status = wa$display_status,
    Sessions_Attended = wa[[sess_att_col]],
    Sessions_Held = wa[[sess_held_col]],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  knitr::kable(df, format = 'html', table.attr = 'class="table table-striped"')
}

```

```{r attendance, echo=FALSE}
# Render attendance table using passed params
render_attendance_table(params$weekly_attendance)
```
